name: Crew
description: Library component. Models a crew member -- gastown's human-in-the-loop pattern (per gastown-src/internal/crew/). Crews are persistent identifiers that survive WorkSource cycles -- they don't have working/done state, they just exist. Per gastown-guide/crew-vs-polecat: 'Crew has states Absent | Created only -- no Working/Idle in the code.' This component models exactly that: a presence-tracker that, once Created, stays Created. Use cases: identifying which human is reviewing what, attaching audit metadata, matching merge approvers. Comparison to Polecat: Polecat has a real lifecycle state machine; Crew doesn't. Modeling them with the same depth would be dishonest.

CREATE.description: receives a creation signal that brings the crew into existence. Tagged 'Create'. Once received, no further transitions.

actors.name: Crew
actors.owner: A crew member. Absent until created; Created forever after. The persistence-across-WorkSource-cycles property is captured by the lack of any Created -> Absent transition.

triples.event: terminal: crew persists forever once created
